data.title: Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. (TSX: IS and IS.PR.A) announces October 2025 distributions for Class A shareholders.
data.description: A distribution for October 2025 will be paid out to Class A shareholders of Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. (TSX: IS and IS.PR.A) with a favorable amount.

data.title: Medpace analyst raises forecasts after Q3 earnings beat. Earnings were $3.86 (consensus $3.52), sales hit $659.9M (consensus $640.99M), up 23.7% YoY.2025 EPS forecast raised from $13.76-$14.53 to $14.60-$14.86. Revenue also surged. More on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Medpace Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: MEDP) announced impressive third-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations. Their earnings of $3.86 exceeded the anticipated $3.52, while sales stood at $659.9 million, higher than the estimated $640.99 million. This marked a significant 23.7% increase in sales from the previous year. Additionally, on a constant currency basis, revenue saw a 23.4% surge. Medpace has consequently heightened its fiscal 2025 earnings per share projection from $13.76-$14.53 to $14.60-$14.86, compared to the consensus of $14.07. Their 2025 sales guidance has also been elevated from $2.42 billion-$2.52 billion to $2.48 billion-$2.53 billion. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Intel Corp. surpassed Q3 revenue expectations, reporting $13.65B, with earnings of 23 cents per share, positively impacting analyst forecasts. CEO Lip-Bu Tan cited AI as a key factor. Q4 revenue expected between $12.8B to...
data.description: Intel Corp. (NASDAQ: INTC) exceeded expectations in the third quarter, with revenue reaching $13.65 billion compared to analyst estimates of $13.14 billion. The chipmaker also surpassed projected earnings, reporting 23 cents per share against the expected one cent per share. CEO Lip-Bu Tan highlighted the role of AI in boosting demand for compute resources, leading to growth opportunities across various product lines including x86 platforms, ASICs, accelerators, and foundry services. Tan emphasized Intel's strong position in CPUs and its U.S.-based manufacturing and R&D capabilities. For the fourth quarter, Intel anticipates revenue to fall within the range of $12.8 billion.

data.title: Smart Oil Crops Market Research Report predicts growth from 2025-2035, driven by demand for sustainable oilseeds, with focus on real-time root-to-oil intelligence & swarm robotics. - DUBLIN
data.description: Dublin - A new report titled "Smart Oil Crops Market - A Global and Regional Analysis: Focus on Application, Product, and Regional Analysis - Analysis and Forecast, 2025-2035" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The smart oil crops market is on a growth trajectory due to the rising need for sustainable, high-yield oilseeds to address global food and biofuel requirements. Demand is steadily increasing as countries aim to lessen dependence on imported edible oils and enhance food security. This report offers insights into the market trends and forecasts for the period 2025-2035.

data.title: Education Cannot Wait investments in Syria support teacher training and continued learning for displaced children; Rudayfah, 40, stands as a symbol of hope in a refugee camp.
data.description: In the midst of a Syrian refugee camp, a dedicated teacher named Rudayfah, aged 40, shines as she leads a classroom filled with hopeful children. Despite the numerous obstacles they encounter, she serves as a beacon of hope, offering them a valuable opportunity to learn. Rudayfah's role goes beyond that of a mere educator; she stands as a symbol of resilience and determination in the face of adversity.

data.title: Saudi Arabia Toothpaste Market valued at USD 193.81M in 2024, expected to reach USD 240.32M by 2030, growing at 3.71% CAGR.
data.description: A new report on the Saudi Arabia Toothpaste Market, available through ResearchAndMarkets.com, forecasts significant growth in the industry. In 2024, the market was valued at USD 193.81 million, with expectations to reach USD 240.32 million by 2030, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 3.71%. Factors contributing to this expansion include heightened awareness of oral hygiene, a surge in disposable incomes, and a shift in consumer preferences towards premium and specialized products.

data.title: GSK stock crumbles post FDA approval for blood cancer treatment Blenrep, falling unexpectedly short.
data.description: Shares of pharmaceutical company GSK experienced a significant decline on Friday following an announcement from the FDA regarding the approval of its blood cancer treatment, Blenrep. The approval was narrower than initially anticipated, resulting in a disappointing outcome for the company. This news impacted investors and caused the stock to plummet. The approval received by Blenrep did not meet the expectations set by the market.

data.title: Ford stock rises despite EV division losing $1.4B due to 'Model T' focus in Q3 earnings, impacting market positively.
data.description: Ford's stock experienced a rise in early stock market trading on Friday after the release of their third-quarter earnings. Despite its electric vehicle division facing a loss of $1.4 billion due to efforts towards a new "Model T" initiative, Ford's stock showed an upward trend.

data.title: Sanofi stock rises on strong Q3 sales and earnings, Dupixent nears $5 billion in sales.
data.description: At the start of Friday trading, Sanofi's stock experienced a significant surge due to surpassing expectations in sales and earnings for the third quarter. This was largely driven by the impressive performance of Dupixent. The sales for Dupixent in the third quarter almost reached $5 billion, leading to the positive market reaction for Sanofi.

data.title: CPI inflation data is favorable for Fed cuts; S&P 500 hits record high; new vehicle prices unaffected by Trump tariffs.
data.description: The current tariffs imposed by the Trump administration have not led to an increase in new vehicle prices thus far. Recent Consumer Price Index (CPI) data indicates a moderate inflation rate, allowing the Federal Reserve more flexibility for interest rate cuts. Additionally, the S&P 500 index has reached a record high, reflecting positive market performance.

data.title: "Beijing launches "Great Wall Hero" 2025 Global Marketing Campaign to boost tourism. Hosted by Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture and Tourism, with Changping District Bureau, aims to attract global attention."
data.description: On October 24, 2025, in Beijing, the "Great Wall Hero" 2025 Global Marketing Campaign was launched by the Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture and Tourism. This campaign is being co-organized by the Changping District Bureau of Culture and Tourism along with the Beijing Changping Cultural Tourism Development Group. This initiative aims to promote the Great Wall internationally, attracting tourists from around the world and boosting tourism numbers in the region.

data.title: AMTD's film "Mother Bhumi" recognized at intl film festivals. AMTD Group Inc. collaborating with TGE, HKD.
data.description: On October 24, 2025, in Paris, New York, and Singapore, AMTD Group Inc. and The Generation Essentials Group revealed news about AMTD's recent film, "Mother Bhumi." This announcement was made in collaboration with TGE, a subsidiary of AMTD Digital. AMTD Group Inc., also known as AMTD, and TGE, traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol TGE. Additionally, AMTD Digital is listed on the New York Stock Exchange using the ticker symbol HKD.

data.title: Procter & Gamble outperforms in Q1 with $1.99 EPS (+3% YoY) and sales of $22.386B, rises stock.
data.description: Procter & Gamble Co. (NYSE: PG) shares surged in early trading post beating first-quarter estimates. They reported first-quarter adjusted earnings per share at $1.99, marking a 3% increase from the previous year, exceeding the analyst consensus of $1.90. Their quarterly sales stood at $22.386 billion, surpassing the Street's projection of $22.175 billion. The company disclosed a 3% rise in quarterly net sales and a 2% increase in organic sales, with higher pricing contributing one percent and favorable mix another percent to this growth. Operating cash flow reached $5.4 billion and net earnings amounted to $4.8 billion, showcasing 102% free cash flow productivity. The Beauty segment witnessed a notable 6% growth in organic sales year over year.

data.title: Integer Holdings Corporation reported Q3 earnings of $1.79/share, beating estimates. Sales: $467.691M vs $466.452M forecasted. CEO optimistic about growth despite some challenges.
data.description: Integer Holdings Corporation (NYSE: ITGR) exceeded expectations in the third quarter as they reported earnings of $1.79 per share, surpassing the estimated $1.68 per share. Their quarterly sales totaled $467.691 million, also beating the consensus estimate of $466.452 million. Joseph Dziedzic, the President and CEO of Integer, highlighted the company's strong growth in sales (up 8%), adjusted operating income (up 14%), and adjusted EPS growth (25%). Despite potential obstacles affecting their 2026 sales, Integer remains confident in their strategy and product development pipeline.
